title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an access control policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

title: Account Management
control_text: The organization manages information system accounts, including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts in accordance with organizational policies.

title: Access Enforcement
control_text: The information system enforces approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

title: Information Flow Enforcement
control_text: The information system enforces approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between connected systems based on information flow control policies.

title: Unsuccessful Logons
control_text: The information system enforces a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a specific time period and automatically locks the account or delays the next logon.

title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an audit and accountability policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, and compliance.

title: Event Logging
control_text: The organization determines that the information system is capable of auditing the following events: user authentication, privilege use, file access, and system configuration changes.

title: Content of Audit Records
control_text: The information system generates audit records containing information that establishes what type of event occurred, when it occurred, where it occurred, and the source of the event.

title: Audit Record Review
control_text: The organization reviews and analyzes information system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity and reports findings to designated officials.

title: Audit Generation
control_text: The information system provides audit record generation capability for the audit events defined by the organization at all components where audit capability is required.

title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a configuration management policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, and compliance.

title: Baseline Configuration
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and maintains under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the information system.

title: Configuration Change Control
control_text: The organization determines the types of changes to the information system that are configuration controlled and reviews proposed configuration changes.

title: Least Functionality
control_text: The organization configures the information system to provide only essential capabilities and prohibits or restricts the use of non-essential functions, ports, and protocols.

title: System Component Inventory
control_text: The organization develops and documents an inventory of information system components that accurately reflects the current system and includes all components within the authorization boundary.

title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an identification and authentication policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, and compliance.

title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
control_text: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates organizational users or processes acting on behalf of organizational users.

title: Authenticator Management
control_text: The organization manages information system authenticators by establishing and maintaining initial authenticator content and ensuring that authenticators have sufficient strength.

title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
control_text: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates non-organizational users or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users.

title: Re-authentication
control_text: The information system requires users to re-authenticate when changing authenticators or after a defined period of time or event.

title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates an incident response policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, and compliance.

title: Incident Handling
control_text: The organization implements an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.

title: Incident Reporting
control_text: The organization requires personnel to report suspected security incidents to the incident response capability within the defined time period.

title: Incident Response Plan
control_text: The organization develops an incident response plan that provides a roadmap for implementing the incident response capability and describes the structure of the response team.

title: Incident Response Support
control_text: The organization provides an incident response support resources, including the ability to communicate with incident response personnel and technical experts.

title: Policy and Procedures
control_text: The organization develops, documents, and disseminates a system and communications protection policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, and responsibilities.

title: Denial of Service Protection
control_text: The information system protects against or limits the effects of denial of service attacks by employing boundary protection devices and resource management.

title: Boundary Protection
control_text: The information system monitors and controls communications at the external boundary of the system and at key internal boundaries within the system.

title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
control_text: The information system protects the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information using cryptographic mechanisms or protected secondary communication paths.

title: Cryptographic Protection
control_text: The information system implements cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and to detect unauthorized modification during transmission.
